contentBlocks.title: Why convert TIFF to PDF?
contentBlocks.html:
TIFF is the standard for professional imaging and archival storage, but it cannot be opened easily on most devices — Windows requires specialized software, and web browsers do not support TIFF natively. Converting TIFF to PDF creates a universally readable document that opens on every device and platform.

TIFF to PDF conversion is essential for sharing scanned documents, medical images, architectural plans, and archival photos. Converting multiple TIFF files into a single multi-page PDF simplifies distribution and preserves the original image quality.

This converter processes files locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server. No registration, no limits, no watermarks.

contentBlocks.title: Quality settings - what to choose when converting TIFF to PDF?
contentBlocks.html:
The quality slider lets you set a value from 60% to 95%. Higher values mean better image quality but larger file sizes.

  • 85% (default) - a good balance for most use cases: business websites, blogs, articles. The visual difference compared to the original is virtually unnoticeable.
  • 85–90% - for product photography, portfolios, and galleries where quality is the priority.
  • 60–70% - when minimizing file size is the priority, e.g. thumbnails in category grids.

At 85% quality, the visual difference between the original TIFF and the resulting PDF is virtually imperceptible to the naked eye, while the file is significantly smaller.

contentBlocks.title: Tips for converting TIFF to PDF
contentBlocks.description: A few tips to help you avoid common issues during conversion:

items.title: Very large files
items.description: Converting images above 4000×4000 pixels may be slower and strain the browser. If processing many large files, consider splitting them into batches of 10–20.

items.title: Already compressed files
items.description: If the original TIFF file was already heavily compressed, converting to PDF may not yield significant size savings. Try different quality settings.

items.title: Transparency
items.description: The PDF format does not support transparency. Transparent areas will be replaced with a white background. Consider PNG or WebP for transparency.

items.title: Keep your originals
items.description: Lossy conversion is irreversible. Keep copies of original TIFF files so you can re-convert with different settings if needed.

contentBlocks.title: How much can you save by converting TIFF to PDF?
contentBlocks.html:
Savings depend on the source file type and its original compression. Below are example results:

Camera photo

12 MB → 890 KB

Saving: ~93%

Product image

5 MB → 320 KB

Saving: ~94%

Screenshot / banner

3.5 MB → 180 KB

Saving: ~95%

Actual savings may vary depending on image content and quality settings. The converter shows the exact size before and after conversion for each file.

contentBlocks.title: How image conversion impacts page speed and SEO
contentBlocks.html:
TIFF to PDF conversion is commonly used for document archival and sharing. TIFF files cannot be viewed in web browsers or opened easily on most devices, while PDF is universally readable.

When sharing PDFs online, file size matters. TIFF files are large (10–50 MB each), but the converter compresses images within the PDF to produce more manageable file sizes. A 10-page PDF from TIFF scans will typically be 10–30 MB.

For web display, consider converting TIFF directly to JPG, WebP, or AVIF instead. These formats produce much smaller files optimized for browser display.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about TIFF to PDF conversion

items.question: Is converting TIFF to PDF free?
items.answer: Yes, this converter is completely free with no limits. No registration, no watermarks.

items.question: Does the converter upload my files?
items.answer: No. All processing happens locally in your browser. Your files never leave your device.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
items.answer: Yes, the converter supports batch conversion. Add multiple files and convert them all simultaneously.

items.question: Does the converter work on mobile?
items.answer: Yes, the converter works on any device with a modern web browser, including smartphones and tablets.

items.question: Is converting TIFF to PDF safe for my files?
items.answer: Yes. All conversion happens locally in your browser using the Canvas API. Files are never sent to any external server. When you close the page, data is automatically cleared.

items.question: Can I set the page order in the PDF?
items.answer: Yes. TIFF files are added to the PDF in the order you upload them. You can rearrange the order before conversion by dragging files in the list.

items.question: Is PDF supported by all browsers?
items.answer: The PDF format is widely supported by modern browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, and Opera.
